name: Kuba Raffia Cloth
primary_sources: Vanessa Drake Moraga's art history analysis

visual_attributes.morphology: rectangular or square pieces of woven cloth
visual_attributes.texture: dense and plush with a velvet-like texture

domain_specific_visual.GEOMETRIC_PATTERN: rectilinear lines and motifs like guilloche interlace

domain_specific_functional.CULTURAL_EXPRESSION: represents community values and artistic skill
domain_specific_functional.MAGICAL_PROPERTIES: twool dye believed to have protective properties

relationships.description: Created by the Kuba people of the Democratic Republic of the Congo
Embroidered and decorated by women, often pregnant, in the Kuba community
Designs inspired by Kuba beliefs in spells, witchcraft, and the supernatural

contextual_attributes.historical_period: ongoing, with roots in early 20th century and earlier
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents Kuba cultural values, community, and artistic expression

contrastive_constraints: NOT devoid of cultural or ceremonial context
